description: Maps every paper claim to concrete evidence in the repository.

claims.detail: summary.by_category has 12 keys: arithmetic (49), division (21), shift (17), loops (8), error_handling (8), bitwise (29), string (2), memory (6), cast (13), control_flow (11), real_patterns (26), compound (12)

claims.claim: 8 UB-related divergences invisible to differential testing
claims.detail: Config A (full pipeline with σ-bridge) achieves 80% accuracy vs Config B (no σ-bridge) at 40% accuracy on 10 pairs. Config C (random testing 10K samples) matches Config A at 80% but misses UB divergences like saturating_add. See also pbt_baseline_results.json: semrec_only_found includes 'saturating_add'.

claims.claim: CEGAR convergence: 16.7% on 30 functions, 0% for UB functions
claims.detail: cegar_nano.analysis: total_pairs=30, converged=5 (16.7%), repair_by_class.undefined_behavior: total=6, repaired=0. All 5 convergences were equivalent_on_first_try.

claims.claim: Scaled CEGAR: 16.3% on 215 functions, 0% for overflow category
claims.detail: cegar.statistics: total=215, success=35, success_rate=0.163. by_category.overflow: total=30, success=0, rate=0.0

claims.claim: σ-bridge halves the error rate (80% → 40% without it)
claims.detail: summary: config_a_accuracy=0.8 (full SemRec with σ-bridge), config_b_accuracy=0.4 (no σ-bridge, plain BV semantics), config_c_accuracy=0.8 (random testing 10K samples). 10 pairs tested.

claims.claim: Conditional Soundness: UNSAT implies equivalence on non-UB inputs
claims.detail: For functions in fragment F: Π(f_C, f_R) = UNSAT ⟹ ∀x ∈ D_¬UB. f_C(x) = f_R(x)

claims.claim: Counterexample Correctness: SAT model is a genuine divergence witness
claims.detail: If Π(f_C, f_R) = SAT with model x₀, then either output divergence (x₀ ∈ D_¬UB) or UB divergence (x₀ ∉ D_¬UB)

claims.claim: Decidability of verification in supported fragment
claims.detail: QF_BV is decidable; loop unrolling at depth K produces finite formula; solver always terminates modulo timeout

claims.claim: UB Divergence Irreparability: C UB cannot be semantically preserved in safe Rust
claims.detail: For C function with UB on input x₀, any Rust translation either produces a well-defined value (semantic divergence) or panics (operational divergence). Translation is divergent at x₀ in either case.

claims.claim: Verification Oracle API returns (verdict, counterexample, repair_hint) triples
claims.detail: VerificationOracle.verify() returns OracleResult dataclass with verdict, counterexample (CounterexampleInfo), and repair_hint (RepairHint) fields

claims.claim: CLI with verify, cegar, bench commands
claims.detail: Three subcommands registered via argparse: verify (line 49), cegar (line 62), bench (line 72)

claims.claim: σ-bridge encodes C vs Rust semantic differences
claims.detail: SemanticConfig class with presets c11(), rust_release(), rust_debug(). Parameterizes overflow, division, shift, cast semantics.

claims.claim: Product program construction aligns C and Rust IR
claims.detail: ProductBuilder, alignment.py, coercion.py, normalizer.py implement product program with coercion points

claims.claim: C and Rust parsers to typed SSA IR
claims.detail: Hand-written recursive descent parsers. ir_lowering.py lowers parsed AST to shared SSA IR.

claims.claim: SMT encoding to QF_BV via Z3
claims.detail: SMT encoder lowers product program to quantifier-free bitvector formulas. Z3 solver interface with configurable timeout.
Average 138.4ms across 351 pairs in initial benchmark
